title: Whitney Wolfe Herd
text:
PROFILE

# Whitney Wolfe Herd

Founder, Bumble

$400M

### 2024 America's Self-Made Women Net Worth

as of 5/28/24

   

Photo by Guerin Blask for Forbes

From the Editor

## Last Updated May 28, 2024, 1:01am EDT

* Whitney Wolfe Herd is cofounder of Bumble, which operates online dating apps Bumble and Badoo. In March 2025 she became CEO again, after stepping down in early 2024.
* Wolfe Herd founded Bumble, the dating app on which women make the first move, with Russian billionaire Andrey Andreev in 2014.
* Andreev, who founded Badoo in 2004, sold his stake in both apps to private equity firm Blackstone in November 2019.
* Wolfe Herd, who owns a 18% stake in Bumble, became the youngest self-made woman billionaire after it went public in February 2021--for ten months.
* Together, Bumble and Badoo operate in around 150 countries with 4 million paying users as of December 2023.

Did you know

In 2012, Wolfe Herd cofounded dating app Tinder. She left the company and sued over sexual harassment. Tinder denied any wrongdoing. The parties eventually settled.

When Wolfe Herd first set out to found Bumble, she wanted it to be a women's social platform, not a dating app.

In Their Own Words

> The power lunch is no longer just for men. We all deserve a seat at the table.
> 
> Whitney Wolfe Herd

[### Bumble Founder Whitney Wolfe Herd Is Looking Ahead ‘Decades Not Days’ After The Dating App’s $2.2 Billion IPO](https://www.forbes.com/sites/katherinelove/2021/10/12/bumble-founder-whitney-wolfe-herd-is-looking-ahead-decades-not-days-after-the-dating-apps-22-billion-ipo/)

The 32-year-old spoke about entrepreneurship and the Austin-based company’s growth at the Forbes Under 30 Summit.

By [Katherine Love](https://www.forbes.com/sites/katherinelove/) Forbes Staff
